function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

{"content": "     1|---
     2|name: binance-trading
     3|description: Binance spot and futures trading via python-requests — API setup, order types, WebSocket streaming, and common pitfalls. No official SDK required.
     4|---
     5|
     6|# Binance Trading Skill
     7|
     8|## Trigger
     9|Use when the user wants to trade on Binance via Python script (no official SDK), need spot/futures data, or want to set up automated trading on Binance.
    10|
    11|## Authentication
    12|
    13|### API Key Setup
    14|1. Go to [Binance API Management](https://www.binance.com/en/my/settings/api-management)
    15|2. Create a new API key (System-generated or manually entered)
    16|3. For futures: enable "Enable Futures" permission
    17|4. Store keys securely — never hardcode
    18|
    19|```python
    20|import os
    21|import requests
    22|
    23|API_KEY=os.env...EY"]
    24|API_SECRET=os.env...ET"]
    25|```
    26|
    27|## Spot vs Futures Endpoints
    28|
    29|### Spot (No Auth for Public Data)
    30|| Purpose | URL |
    31||---------|-----|
    32|| Test connectivity | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/ping` |
    33|| Exchange info | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/exchangeInfo` |
    34|| Klines | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/klines?symbol=BTCUSDT&interval=1h&limit=500` |
    35|| 24hr ticker | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/ticker/24hr?symbol=BTCUSDT` |
    36|| Order book | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/depth?symbol=BTCUSDT&limit=100` |
    37|| My trades | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/myTrades?symbol=BTCUSDT` |
    38|| Place order (SIGNED) | `POST https://api.binance.com/api/v3/order` |
    39|
    40|### Spot Authenticated Requests
    41|```python
    42|import time
    43|import hashlib
    44|import requests
    45|
    46|def binance_spot_request(method: str, endpoint: str, params: dict = None):
    47|    """Sign and send authenticated Binance Spot API request."""
    48|    params = params or {}
    49|    params["timestamp"] = int(time.time() * 1000)
    50|    params["recvWindow"] = 5000
    51|
    52|    # Build query string and sign with HMAC SHA256
    53|    query = "&".join([f"{k}={v}" for k, v in params.items()])
    54|    signature = hashlib.sha256((API_SECRET + query).encode()).hexdigest()
    55|    params["signature"] = signature
    56|
    57|    headers = {"X-MBX-APIKEY": API_KEY}
    58|    url = f"https://api.binance.com{endpoint}"
    59|
    60|    if method == "GET":
    61|        return requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
    62|    elif method == "POST":
    63|        return requests.post(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
    64|    elif method == "DELETE":
    65|        return requests.delete(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
    66|```
    67|
    68|### Futures API Endpoints
    69|| Purpose | URL |
    70||---------|-----|
    71|| Futures exchange info | `GET https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/exchangeInfo` |
    72|| Klines | `GET https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/klines?symbol=BTCUSDT&interval=1h&limit=500` |
    73|| Account info (SIGNED) | `GET https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v2/account` |
    74|| Place order (SIGNED) | `POST https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/order` |
    75|| Position mode (SIGNED) | `POST https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/positionSide/dual` |
    76|
    77|### Futures Signed Request
    78|```python
    79|import hmac
    80|import time
    81|
    82|def binance_futures_request(method: str, endpoint: str, params: dict = None):
    83|    """Sign and send authenticated Binance Futures API request."""
    84|    params = params or {}
    85|    params["timestamp"] = int(time.time() * 1000)
    86|    params["recvWindow"] = 5000
    87|
    88|    query = "&".join([f"{k}={v}" for k, v in params.items()])
    89|    signature = hmac.new(API_SECRET.encode(), query.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
    90|    params["signature"] = signature
    91|
    92|    headers = {"X-MBX-APIKEY": API_KEY}
    93|    base = "https://fapi.binance.com"
    94|    url = f"{base}{endpoint}"
    95|

```python
    while True:
```

```python
def classify_whale(symbol, amount):
    """Classify transaction size."""
    thresholds = WHALE_THRESHOLDS.get(symbol, 1_000_000)
    if amount >= thresholds * 10:
        return "MEGA WHALE"  # 10x threshold
    elif amount >= thresholds * 5:
        return "LARGE WHALE"
    elif amount >= thresholds:
        return "WHALE"
    else:
        return "NOTABLE"
```

```python
def detect_exchange_flow(tx_from, tx_to):
    """Detect if whale is moving to/from exchanges (potential sell signal)."""
    from_lower = tx_from.lower()
    to_lower = tx_to.lower()
    
    for exchange, addrs in EXCHANGE_ADDRESSES.items():
        if from_lower in addrs:
            return f"OUTFLOW from {exchange.upper()}"
        if to_lower in addrs:

    return None
```

```python
def format_whale_alert(tx):
    """Format whale alert for display."""
    blockchain = tx.get("blockchain", "").upper()
    symbol = tx.get("symbol", "")
    amount = tx.get("amount", 0)
    usd = tx.get("usd", 0)
```

# Funding Rate & Liquidations Skill

## Trigger
Use when user wants to:
- Monitor funding rates to detect market sentiment (bullish vs bearish)
- Track liquidation levels for key crypto assets
- Identify funding rate divergences between exchanges
- Get alerts when funding rates become extreme
- Analyze historical funding rate patterns for mean-reversion signals

## Core Concepts

### Funding Rates
- **Positive funding** → Longs pay shorts (bearish sentiment)
- **Negative funding** → Shorts pay longs (bullish sentiment)
- **Extreme funding** (>0.1% per 8h) often signals top/bottom zones

### Liquidations
- **Long liquidations** → Price drops sharply, stop-hunts below key levels
- **Short liquidations** → Price rises rapidly, short-squeeze scenarios
- **Concentration** of liquidations at one level = potential support/resistance

## Data Sources

### Binance Futures
```python
import requests

def get_binance_funding_rate(symbol="BTCUSDT"):
    """Get current funding rate for Binance futures."""
    url = "https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/premiumIndex"
    r = requests.get(url, params={"symbol": symbol}, timeout=10)
    data = r.json()
    return {
        "symbol": data["symbol"],
        "funding_rate": float(data["lastFundingRate"]) * 100,  # as percentage
        "next_funding_time": data["nextFundingTime"]
    }
```

## Funding Rate Functions

### 1. Multi-Exchange Funding Monitor

```python
def monitor_all_funding_rates(symbols=["BTC", "ETH", "SOL"]):
    """Collect funding rates from all exchanges."""
    results = {}
    
    # Binance

        except:

    # Bybit

    # Hyperliquid
    for sym in symbols:

            results[f"hyperliquid_{sym}"] = get_hyperliquid_funding(sym)
        except:

    return results
```

### 2. Funding Rate Divergence Detector
```python
def detect_funding_divergence(symbol="BTC"):
    """Find funding rate differences between exchanges."""
    fr = monitor_all_funding_rates([symbol])
    
    rates = []
    for exchange, data in fr.items():
        if "funding_rate" in data:
            rates.append((exchange, data["funding_rate"]))
    
    if len(rates) < 2:
        return None
    
    avg = sum(r for _, r in rates) / len(rates)
    max_diff = max(rates, key=lambda x: abs(x[1] - avg))
    
    return {
        "symbol": symbol,
        "rates": dict(rates),
        "average": avg,
        "divergence": max_diff[1] - avg,
        "signal": "SHORT DIVERGENCE" if max_diff[1] > avg * 1.5 else 
                  "LONG DIVERGENCE" if max_diff[1] < avg * 0.5 else "NEUTRAL"
    }
```

### 3. Funding Rate Alert
```python
def check_funding_alerts(symbol="BTC", threshold=0.1):
    """Alert when funding rate exceeds threshold (absolute % per 8h)."""
    fr_data = get_binance_funding_rate(f"{symbol}USDT")
    rate = abs(fr_data["funding_rate"])
    
    if rate > threshold:
        direction = "BEARISH" if fr_data["funding_rate"] > 0 else "BULLISH"
        return (f"🚨 FUNDING ALERT: {symbol} funding rate "
                f"{fr_data['funding_rate']:.4f}% ({direction})")
    return None
```

## Liquidations Functions

### 1. Real-time Liquidation Tracker
```python
import json
import time

def track_liquidations(symbol="BTC", min_usd=10_000, interval=60):
    """Track liquidations via exchange WebSocket."""
    # Binance futures liquidation stream
    streams = [
        f"{symbol.lower()}usdt@forceOrder"  # Liquidation stream
    ]
    ws_url = f"wss://fstream.binance.com/stream?streams={'/'.join(streams)}"
    
    def on_message(ws, message):
        data = json.loads(message)
        if "data" in data:
            order = data["data"]["o"]
            if float(order["v"]) * float(order["p"]) > min_usd:
                side = "LONG" if order["S"] == "SELL" else "SHORT"
                print(f"💸 LIQUIDATION: {symbol} {side} "
                      f"${float(order['v']) * float(order['p']):,.0f}")
    
    import websocket
    ws = websocket.WebSocketApp(ws_url, on_message=on_message)
    ws.run_forever()
```

### 2. Liquidation Heatmap Generator
```python
def get_liquidation_heatmap(symbol="BTC"):
    """Build price-level liquidation concentration."""
    # 1. Get recent liquidations
    # 2. Bin by price level
    # 3. Return concentration data
    
    liquidations_url = f"https://fapi.binance.com/futures/data/allForceOrders"
    params = {"symbol": f"{symbol}USDT", "limit": 200}
    r = requests.get(liquidations_url, params=params, timeout=10)
    
    data = r.json()
    price_bins = {}
    
    for liq in data:
        price = float(liq["p"])
        qty = float(liq["q"])
        value = qty * price
        bucket = round(price / 100) * 100  # $100 buckets
        
        if bucket not in price_bins:
            price_bins[bucket] = {"long_liq": 0, "short_liq": 0}
        
        if liq["s"] == "SELL":
            price_bins[bucket]["long_liq"] += value
        else:
            price_bins[bucket]["short_liq"] += value
    
    return price_bins
```

## Combined Analysis

### Funding + Liquidations Dashboard
```python
def funding_liquidation_report(symbol="BTC"):
    """Generate combined funding/liquidation report."""
    report = []
    
    # 1. Funding rates across exchanges
    fr = detect_funding_divergence(symbol)
    if fr:
        report.append(f"📊 {symbol} Funding Divergence: {fr['signal']}")
        report.append(f"   Rates: {fr['rates']}")
        report.append(f"   Average: {fr['average']:.4f}%")
    
    # 2. Liquidation levels
    heatmap = get_liquidation_heatmap(symbol)
    if heatmap:
        top_liq_levels = sorted(
            [(p, d) for p, d in heatmap.items() 
             if d["long_liq"] + d["short_liq"] > 1_000_000],
            key=lambda x: x[1]["long_liq"] + x[1]["short_liq"],
            reverse=True
        )[:3]
        
        report.append(f"\n🔥 Top Liquidation Levels:")
        for level, data in top_liq_levels:
            report.append(f"   ${level:,.0f}: Long ${data['long_liq']/1e6:.1f}M | "
                          f"Short ${data['short_liq']/1e6:.1f}M")
    
    return "\n".join(report)
```

```python
def get_dex_buy_sell_pressure(token_address, chain="ethereum"):
    """Calculate buy/sell pressure from DEX swaps."""
    # Use The Graph to get swap data
    query = """
    {
      swaps(where: {
        tokenIn: "%s"
      }, orderBy: timestamp, orderDirection: desc, first: 100) {
        timestamp
        amountIn
        amountOut
        tokenIn { symbol }
        tokenOut { symbol }
      }
    }
    """ % token_address.lower()
    
    r = requests.post(UNISWAP_V2_SUBGRAPH, json={"query": query}, timeout=10)
    swaps = r.json().get("data", {}).get("swaps", [])
    
    buy_volume = sum(float(s["amountOut"]) for s in swaps 
                     if s["tokenIn"]["symbol"] in ["WETH", "USDC", "USDT"])
    sell_volume = sum(float(s["amountIn"]) for s in swaps
                      if s["tokenOut"]["symbol"] in ["WETH", "USDC", "USDT"])
    
    return {
        "buy_volume": buy_volume,
        "sell_volume": sell_volume,
        "pressure_ratio": buy_volume / sell_volume if sell_volume > 0 else float('inf'),
        "signal": "BUY PRESSURE" if buy_volume > sell_volume else "SELL PRESSURE"
    }
```

```python
def calculate_price_impact(pool_address, swap_amount, is_token0_to_token1=True):
    """Calculate price impact of a swap on a Uniswap V2 pool."""
    pool = analyze_pool_liquidity(pool_address)
    if not pool.get("data", {}).get("pools"):
        return None
    
    p = pool["data"]["pools"][0]
    reserve0 = float(p["token0"]["id"].split("-")[0])  # Simplified
    reserve1 = float(p["token1"]["id"].split("-")[1])  # Simplified
    
    # Actual implementation uses reserves from subgraph
    # reserve0, reserve1 = get_reserves(pool_address)
    
    if is_token0_to_token1:
        # Amount out = input * other_reserve / (input + this_reserve)
        amount_out = swap_amount * reserve1 / (swap_amount + reserve0)
        price_impact = abs(swap_amount - amount_out) / swap_amount * 100
    else:
        amount_out = swap_amount * reserve0 / (swap_amount + reserve1)
        price_impact = abs(swap_amount - amount_out) / swap_amount * 100
    
    return {"amount_out": amount_out, "price_impact_pct": price_impact}
```

```python
    CEX_WALLETS = {

        "coinbase": "0x503828976d22510aad0201ac7ec8a870bd60142",

    }
    
    r = requests.get(url, params=params, timeout=10)

    for tx in txs:

        from_addr = tx["from"].lower()

    }
```
